calendar.matters:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND ATTORNEY'S EXPENSE. (PER ORDER SIGNED SEPT.12.2025.)
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Eden Zakay, Esq. 619-255-9047 Zakay Law Group for Plaintiff.
Jeff Gillette, Esq. 949-335-7905 Messner Reeves for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Zakay informs the Court they are not contesting and have two points for clarification. The Court comments re proposed orders, mediator, exercise authority and be vigilant re absent members. Mr. Gillette seeks clarification on the Paragraph 4 of the tentative ruling.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Andrew Ellenburg-Wiley's Motion for Final Approval and Plaintiff's Motion for Attorney's Fees and Attorney's Expenses is OFF CALENDAR AND THE COURT'S 9/15/2025 PRELIMINARY APPROVAL ORDER IS VACATED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Nov-14-2025 Continued To Feb-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND ATTORNEY'S EXPENSE.

Plaintiff Andrew Ellenburg-Wiley's Motion for Final Approval of Calss Action and PAGA Settlement and for Attorney's Fees and Cost's is, per stipulation, CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 3, 2026, AT 9 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT.

Plaintiff shall file a properly noticed, served, and supported motion for final approval, etc. consistent with CCP and CRC requirements set for hearing on February 3, 2026.

Moving Defendant must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
